 {"id":"bd-92e1","title":"Add assignment tracking - ntm status --assignments shows which agent has which bead","description":"## Overview
Track which agent has which bead assigned throughout the session lifecycle.

## Data Model
```go
type AssignmentStore struct {
    SessionName string
    Assignments map[string]*Assignment  // bead_id -> assignment
    mutex       sync.RWMutex
}

type Assignment struct {
    BeadID      string
    BeadTitle   string
    Pane        int
    AgentType   string    // claude, codex, gemini
    AgentName   string    // Agent Mail name if registered
    Status      AssignmentStatus
    AssignedAt  time.Time
    StartedAt   *time.Time  // When agent started working
    CompletedAt *time.Time
    FailedAt    *time.Time
    FailReason  string
    PromptSent  string    // The actual prompt sent
}

type AssignmentStatus string
const (
    StatusAssigned   AssignmentStatus = "assigned"   // Prompt sent, waiting to start
    StatusWorking    AssignmentStatus = "working"    // Agent actively working
    StatusCompleted  AssignmentStatus = "completed"  // Bead closed successfully
    StatusFailed     AssignmentStatus = "failed"     // Agent crashed or gave up
    StatusReassigned AssignmentStatus = "reassigned" // Moved to different agent
)
```

## Persistence
- File: .ntm/<session>/assignments.json
- Format: JSON array of Assignment objects
- Auto-save: On every state change
- Auto-load: On session attach/resume

## State Transitions
```
assigned -> working (agent starts output)
assigned -> failed (agent crashes before starting)
working -> completed (bead closed in br)
working -> failed (agent crashes/gives up)
working -> reassigned (manual reassignment)
failed -> assigned (retry to same or different agent)
```

## Error Handling
```go
type PersistenceError struct {
    Operation string  // "save", "load", "migrate"
    Path      string
    Cause     error
}

// Error scenarios and handling:
//
// 1. File write failure (disk full, permissions)
//    - Keep in-memory state intact
//    - Log error: "[ASSIGN] Failed to persist: %v"
//    - Retry on next state change
//    - Return error to caller (non-fatal for operations)
//
// 2. File read failure (corrupted JSON)
//    - Try to recover from backup: assignments.json.bak
//    - If no backup: Start fresh, log warning
//    - Log: "[ASSIGN] Corrupted state, recovered from backup"
//
// 3. Session directory doesn't exist
//    - Create .ntm/<session>/ automatically
//    - Log: "[ASSIGN] Created session directory"
//
// 4. Concurrent access race
//    - All methods are mutex-protected
//    - If lock times out (5s), return error
//    - Caller can retry
//
// 5. Invalid state transition
//    - Return error but don't panic
//    - Log: "[ASSIGN] Invalid transition %s -> %s for bd-%s"
//    - Leave current state unchanged
//
// 6. Assignment for non-existent bead
//    - Allow creation (bead may be created later)
//    - Mark with warning flag
//    - Log: "[ASSIGN] Warning: bd-%s not found in workspace"
```

## Backup Strategy
```go
func (s *AssignmentStore) Save() error {
    // 1. Write to temporary file
    tmpPath := s.path + ".tmp"
    
    // 2. If temp write succeeds, rename current to .bak
    bakPath := s.path + ".bak"
    os.Rename(s.path, bakPath)
    
    // 3. Rename temp to current
    os.Rename(tmpPath, s.path)
    
    // 4. Keep last backup (don't delete .bak)
}

func (s *AssignmentStore) Load() error {
    data, err := os.ReadFile(s.path)
    if err != nil {
        // Try backup
        data, err = os.ReadFile(s.path + ".bak")
        if err != nil {
            return nil // Start fresh
        }
    }
    return json.Unmarshal(data, &s.Assignments)
}
```

## Integration Points
- ntm assign: Creates assignments, persists immediately
- ntm status --assignments: Shows current assignment map
- Completion detector: Updates status to completed/failed
- ntm spawn --assign: Creates assignments after spawn

## Commands
```bash
ntm status myproject --assignments           # Human-readable table
ntm status myproject --assignments --json    # JSON for scripts
ntm robot-status myproject                   # Includes assignments in robot output
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AssignmentStore persists to JSON file
- [ ] Loads assignments on session attach
- [ ] Status transitions work correctly
- [ ] ntm status --assignments shows table
- [ ] JSON output includes all fields
- [ ] Thread-safe for concurrent updates
- [ ] Handles agent crash gracefully
- [ ] Integrates with completion detector
- [ ] Recovers from corrupted state file
- [ ] Creates session directory if missing
- [ ] Validates state transitions
- [ ] Maintains backup file

## Tests Required
- Unit test: CRUD operations on AssignmentStore
- Unit test: State transitions (all valid paths)
- Unit test: Invalid state transition handling
- Unit test: Persistence save/load cycle
- Unit test: Concurrent access safety
- Unit test: Recovery from corrupted file
- Unit test: Recovery from missing directory
- E2E test: Full assignment lifecycle with real agents","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:32:34.806492530Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T22:49:42.840966121Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T22:49:42.840868898Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-92e1","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-96jt","title":"Task: Implement .beads/ initialization","description":"## Purpose
Initialize beads issue tracking via br init if available.

## Scope
- Check if br binary is available in PATH
- If available, run br init in project directory
- If not available, log warning and continue
- Handle case where .beads/ already exists

## Acceptance Criteria
- .beads/ created via br init when br is available
- Graceful degradation when br not installed
- Warning message suggests installing beads_rust
- Does not fail if .beads/ already exists

## Technical Notes
Use exec.LookPath to check for br binary
Capture stdout/stderr for error reporting","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:28:56.761623587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T07:41:13.867298441Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T07:41:13.867251823Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-96jt","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-972v","title":"Add ntm context-inject - inject AGENTS.md and README.md content into first prompt automatically","description":"Add ntm context-inject command - inject AGENTS.md and README.md content into first prompt automatically.

## Problem Being Solved

Agents often need project context (AGENTS.md, README.md) to understand the codebase. Manually including this is tedious.

## Command Design

```bash
ntm context-inject SESSION [--files FILE_LIST] [--max-tokens N]
```

### Behavior
1. Reads AGENTS.md and README.md from project root
2. Formats content with clear section headers
3. Sends to pane 1 (controller) or all panes with --all

### Default Files
- AGENTS.md (if exists)
- README.md (if exists)
- .claude/project_context.md (if exists)

### Options
- --files: Comma-separated list of files to inject
- --max-tokens: Truncate if content exceeds N tokens
- --all: Inject to all panes, not just pane 1

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-context-inject=SESSION [--files=FILE_LIST]

Returns JSON:
```json
{
  "session": "my_session",
  "injected_files": ["AGENTS.md", "README.md"],
  "total_tokens": 1500,
  "panes_injected": [1]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. ntm context-inject command exists and shows in help
2. Reads AGENTS.md and README.md by default
3. Content formatted with ### headers for each file
4. --files flag overrides default file list
5. --max-tokens truncates with '...(truncated)' message
6. --all flag sends to all panes
7. Robot mode returns JSON with injection details

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/cmd/context_inject_test.go
- Test file reading and formatting
- Test token counting and truncation

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-CONTEXT] ntm context-inject Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-CONTEXT]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing ntm context-inject"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. ntm context-inject injects AGENTS.md content
2. README.md content also injected
3. Works with first prompt automatically
4. Config option to enable/disable
5. Supports custom file list
6. Unit tests for injection logic
7. E2E test verifying context appears in agent","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:40:42.503574636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:03:06.597486507Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-99a2i","title":"Tier 3: Observability Layer","description":"EPIC for Tier 3 integrations focused on observability and monitoring.

## Why Tier 3 is Third Priority

After safety (Tier 1) and performance (Tier 2), observability helps understand what's happening:

1. **rano**: Network-level visibility into agent API calls. See which agents are making requests, latency, bytes transferred. Essential for debugging and optimization.

2. **process_triage**: Process-level health monitoring. Detect stuck or zombie agents using Bayesian classification. Know when an agent has gone silent.

## Value Proposition

Without observability:
- Black box: agents are doing... something
- Debug by reading pane output manually
- Zombie agents consume resources silently

## Success Criteria

1. rano integration provides per-agent network stats
2. process_triage detects stuck agents with 95% accuracy
3. Dashboard shows real-time agent health
4. Robot mode exposes all observability data
5. All components have unit tests (80% coverage)
6. E2E tests verify full observability stack
7. Documentation covers all observability features","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:12:54.925171761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:01:25.514074983Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-99a2i","depends_on_id":"bd-3fg81","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-9l7cp","title":"Task: Implement terse profile selection and key mapping","description":"## Overview

- Size reduction measurable in tests
- Existing `--robot-terse` behavior unchanged","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureMoose","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:15:07.944889374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T16:39:59.453899521Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T16:39:59.453831305Z","close_reason":"Verified implemented: terse key map + verbosity profile active for JSON/TOON, --robot-terse unchanged; go test ./internal/robot passes","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9l7cp","depends_on_id":"bd-2tb5i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-9l7cp","depends_on_id":"bd-4uecx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
